name: Haven Savings Bank
description_short: Haven Savings Bank is a New Jersey-based community bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, credit cards, and business banking services with both personal and commercial lending options.
description_long:
Haven Savings Bank is a regional financial institution headquartered in Hoboken, New Jersey, with an additional business development office in New Providence. The bank serves both personal and business customers across northern New Jersey and maintains NMLS licensing (#663144) for mortgage lending operations. Founded to serve the local community, Haven positions itself as a practical banking alternative emphasizing flexible solutions and personalized service.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking and savings accounts with cashback rewards, money market accounts (Step-Up Money Market Account currently offering 3.00% APY with $25,000 balance requirement), CDs, IRAs, and personal credit cards (Haven Visa). On the lending side, Haven provides residential mortgages with both fixed and adjustable rate options—currently advertising a 30-year fixed at 6.250% (6.332% APR) and 5/1 ARM at 5.750% (6.284% APR). Mobile banking and online account management are available to all customers.

For business customers, Haven distinguishes itself through dedicated commercial lending services including real estate loans, business loans, and commercial lines of credit. The bank also offers escrow management, cash management, property management services, and business credit cards. Their New Providence location specifically handles business development and commercial loan origination, indicating a structured approach to small-to-medium business banking.

Haven's primary limitation is geographic reach—the bank operates only in northern New Jersey with two physical locations. As a smaller regional bank, they may not compete on rates or promotional offers compared to national banks or online-only institutions. Their current mortgage rates (6.25-5.75%) and deposit rates (3.00% APY) are competitive but not exceptionally high. The bank does not advertise any special programs for credit-challenged borrowers or alternative lending products, positioning it as a traditional mainstream banking option rather than a niche lender. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with cashback rewards
Savings and money market accounts (Step-Up Money Market Account)
Personal Visa credit cards with rewards
Residential mortgages (fixed and adjustable rate, no-point options)
Mobile banking and online account management
Commercial lending (real estate loans, business loans, lines of credit)
pros: Cashback rewards on checking account transactions—explicitly advertised as a primary checking account feature
Step-Up Money Market Account with 3.00% APY, offering above-average savings rates for accessible money market products
Dedicated commercial lending team with separate New Providence office focused on business loans and commercial real estate
Free mobile banking service with secure account access and management capabilities
Escrow and property management services available, appealing to real estate investors and business owners
No-point mortgage options advertised, reducing upfront closing costs on home loans
Dual personal and business credit card offerings (Haven Visa) with customizable features
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only two physical locations in northern New Jersey, restricting accessibility for customers outside the region
Mortgage rates (6.25-6.33% APR) and deposit rates (3.00% APY) are competitive but not exceptional compared to national banks and online lenders
Minimum balance requirements ($25,000) on money market account to earn advertised APY, excluding lower-balance savers
No advertised special products for credit-challenged borrowers, limited first-time homebuyer programs mentioned
Relatively limited online presence and digital marketing—website is functional but basic compared to competitors
best_for: Northern New Jersey residents and businesses seeking relationship-based community banking with personalized service
Small-to-medium business owners needing commercial real estate loans, business lines of credit, or escrow services
Real estate investors interested in property management services and commercial lending with dedicated loan officers
diagnosis: Haven Savings Bank is best for northern New Jersey residents and small business owners who value personalized service and relationship banking from a local institution. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank operates only in New Jersey with two locations, making it inaccessible for out-of-state customers seeking their services online, and rates/terms are competitive but not market-leading compared to national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
